tweets.text:
Ten years on Ethereum, zero oracle hacks. 

@nomos_paradox tells @Steven_Ehrlich that record is no accident. It is what happens when a team treats security like a religion, not a checklist. 🔐

Worth a listen for anyone lending against tokenized funds.

Timestamps:
🎙️ 00:41 Niklas Kunkel on what actually separates the oracle providers, and why most people get it wrong
🛠️ 02:39 The origin story: why building MakerDAO forced Niklas to invent the oracle from scratch
⚠️ 06:35 The hidden single point of failure lurking inside every DeFi protocol
🏦 11:07 Chronicle's bet: from pricing tokens to verifying the real collateral behind tokenized RWAs
🧾 15:30 Don't trust, verify: how Chronicle rebuilds BlackRock's BUIDL fund from scratch, independently
📣 24:50 Cape: Get 33% off your first six months with code unchained at https://t.co/2ZtLziXsHV
📈 27:07 The hottest RWA trade nobody's talking about: why CLOs like Galaxy's new tokenized credit product are taking off
🚀 32:36 The SpaceX pre-IPO token mess: buyers paid up, then never got their shares
🌉 36:21 The KelpDAO exploit and the uncomfortable truth: every bridge is just an oracle in disguise
📊 42:28 Chronicle's next growth wave: tokenized equities and the redemption gap that could break them
⛓️ 48:15 The counterintuitive one: why even fully onchain-issued funds still can't skip proof of asset

tweets.text:
Iran said Saturday that it’s closing the Strait of Hormuz, saying no vessels will be allowed to pass through until foreign interference ends, according state-run IRIB News.

The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps also said it fired a warning shot on a cargo ship that tried to transit the strait on Saturday despite warnings, the outlet added.

>offer a bunch of liquidity above
>price goes up
>now there’s bid skew underneath

thread_tweets.full_text:
@MoneyPrinter0x without looking in detail, this is very likely wrong. It’s skewed because price is up

>offer a bunch of liquidity above
>price goes up
>now there’s bid skew underneath 

would have to check the adds to confirm, but this is usually the case.

thread_tweets.text:
you'd have to separate out the non-MEV transactions (ie, 3rd/4th one in this list are only LPing for one block, but the 2nd tx is a real single-sided add when price was ~.09)

without looking further i'd say there is a >90% chance this is what happened (ask skew from early buyers trying to exit while receiving the high fees instead of paying them + the slippage+hurting the chart turned bid skew after their asks were mostly eaten)

tweets.full_text:
@MoneyPrinter0x without looking in detail, this is very likely wrong. It’s skewed because price is up

>offer a bunch of liquidity above
>price goes up
>now there’s bid skew underneath 

would have to check the adds to confirm, but this is usually the case.

you'd have to separate out the non-MEV transactions (ie, 3rd/4th one in this list are only LPing for one block, but the 2nd tx is a real single-sided add when price was ~.09)

without looking further i'd say there is a >90% chance this is what happened (ask skew from early buyers trying to exit while receiving the high fees instead of paying them + the slippage+hurting the chart turned bid skew after their asks were mostly eaten)

tweets.text:
From the FT article on bonds issued by tech companies.
As I argued in my FT column four weeks ago (linked below), a traditional "sources and uses" analysis suggests that, other things being equal, yields will have to widen for the market to absorb the massive pipeline of planned debt issuance—not only from tech companies but from governments and othe companies as well.
One of the underlying dynamics will increasingly be that of displacement:
investors selling existing holdings to make room for new ones.
We saw this play out with Amazon this week, though the new $25 billion issue traded disappointingly for its investors.
https://t.co/oYNTskZfrt
#economy #markets #bonds #investors #tech @ft
tweets.full_text:

One of the underlying dynamics will increasingly be that of displacement:

We saw this play out with Amazon this week, though the new $25 billion issue traded disappointingly for its investors.

tweets.text:
A government that borrows in its own currency rarely has to default. It can always print instead.  

Total global debt has climbed above 300% of GDP, and the cost of carrying it rises as yields climb. Cutting spending is politically punishing and printing has become the way out.  

Money created faster than the economy grows buys less over time. Households feel the debasement with the rising cost of living.  

Gold and Bitcoin draw investors partly because no government can print more of them.

(Original content provided by @PonderingDurian at Delphi Ventures)

tweets.text:
Apparently, Korean Beauty was where all the alpha is.

APR (278470.KS) is up 529% over the past 2Y, wildly outperforming both $NVDA and $INTC.

Every female I know talks about their "Medicube" stick, then buying products... where you put a whole salmon's family tree on your face for better skin. 

The world's female population creates a bigger structural demand than hyperscalers for memory or lasers? Should have known...
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
@Liberty0918 반도체 쏟아붓는 와중에 K뷰티가 또 터지네. 1Q 순익 41% 폭증에 하반기 대장주 예고라. 

수출 150억 달러 시대 문턱인데, 미국·유럽 기초 카테고리 중심으로 구조적 성장 중. ODM 쪽 레버리지 제대로 먹는 기업들 영업이익률 30%대 찍는 거 보면 단순 소비재가 아니라 기술+브랜드 모멘텀 섞인

tweets.text:
Sad state of affairs when most mainstream media journalists can’t be trusted to tell the truth at this point.

As a founder, you have to hunt to find the good ones and spend more of your time going direct or with new media, bypassing them entirely.

Many traditional media companies got disrupted by tech, and therefore came to hate tech. So they have a conflict of interest in all tech reporting, even if they don’t disclose it. Others are mainly political propaganda organizations at this point (Marxist etc) and try to label it journalism to prey on ignorant people.

Actual journalism is important and even heroic at times (war zones etc), it just isn’t practiced very often by traditional media companies any more, which is a shame and why we need new media companies.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
I gave WIRED the exclusive on our hands launch, and they wrote a really weird article about how we are sexualizing robotics… 

https://t.co/CSCiVUbz2P

I felt pretty betrayed because that’s not what they told me they were writing about not is that what I’ve ever been about…
tweets.full_text:
Sad state of affairs when most mainstream media journalists can’t be trusted to tell the truth at this point.

As a founder, you have to hunt to find the good ones and spend more of your time going direct or with new media, bypassing them entirely.

Others are mainly political propaganda organizations at this point (Marxist etc) and try to label it journalism to prey on ignorant people.

Actual journalism is important and even heroic at times (war zones etc), it just isn’t practiced very often by traditional media companies any more, which is a shame and why we need new media companies.
